title: Chapter 1 Quiz: Welcome to React Native
description: Test your understanding of React Native basics, setup, JSX, core components, and debugging.

questions.question: What does React Native use to render UI components?
questions.explanation: React Native uses real native components, not a WebView.

questions.question: Which command creates a new Expo project?
questions.explanation: `expo init` is the correct Expo command.

questions.question: What is the correct way to embed a JavaScript variable inside JSX?
questions.explanation: Single curly braces `{}` evaluate JavaScript expressions in JSX.

questions.question: Which core component is used to display text?
questions.explanation: `<Text>` is the only component specifically for text.

questions.question: Which method is generally preferred for performance and organization?
questions.explanation: StyleSheet.create optimises styles and keeps them separate.

questions.question: How do you open the developer menu on a physical device running an Expo app?
questions.explanation: Shaking brings up the dev menu in Expo Go.

questions.question: React Native uses HTML and CSS for styling.
questions.explanation: It uses a subset of CSS with camelCase, but not HTML elements.

questions.question: Expo requires Xcode and Android Studio to be installed.
questions.explanation: Expo abstracts those away; you can develop without them.

questions.question: JSX must always have a single root element.
questions.explanation: Yes, JSX expressions must have one top‑level element.

questions.question: The `<Button>` component in React Native is the same as an HTML `<button>`.
questions.explanation: It renders a native button (UIButton on iOS, android.widget.Button on Android).

questions.question: What does the `flex: 1` style typically do?
options: Makes the component one pixel tall
Makes the component take all available space
questions.explanation: `flex: 1` tells the component to fill the available space.

questions.question: Which property is used to set the image source in the `<Image>` component?
questions.explanation: The prop is `source`, often with an object like `{ uri: '...' }`.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Watchman?
options: To watch for file changes and trigger reloads
questions.explanation: Watchman detects file changes and helps with fast refresh.

questions.question: In React Native, style properties are written in _____ case.
questions.explanation: Use camelCase, e.g., `backgroundColor` instead of `background-color`.

questions.question: The command to start the Expo development server is `expo _____`.
questions.explanation: `expo start` (or `npm start`) launches the bundler.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a core component?
questions.explanation: `<div>` is a web HTML element, not a React Native core component.

questions.question: What does the `onPress` prop do?
options: Handles press events (like a button click)
questions.explanation: `onPress` is the standard prop for handling taps on buttons and touchables.

questions.question: You can use CSS classes like `.my-class` in React Native.
questions.explanation: React Native uses JavaScript objects, not CSS classes.

questions.question: The Metro bundler is responsible for transforming and bundling your JavaScript.
questions.explanation: Metro is the JavaScript bundler used by React Native.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of using Expo?
questions.explanation: Expo abstracts native tooling, making setup easier for beginners.
